signal_title: Code of Practice on Marking and Labelling AI-Generated Content
risk_rationale: The draft bill introduces a new requirement for marking and labelling AI-generated content, which is not currently addressed in TikTok's content moderation policies. This represents a material compliance gap that requires immediate attention to align with the upcoming EU regulations.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Intervention workflows, crisis resources for self-harm/suicide content
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Providers of generative AI systems must mark AI-generated content.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline does not include requirements for marking or labelling AI-generated content, which is mandated by the new EU draft bill.

remediations.title: Implement AI-Generated Content Labelling
remediations.description: Develop and integrate a system to automatically mark and label AI-generated content on the platform, ensuring compliance with the EU's Code of Practice on AI-generated content transparency.
acceptance_criteria: AI-generated content is consistently marked across the platform
Labels for AI-generated content are visible to users in the EU

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new EU regulations could result in significant fines and damage to TikTok's reputation in the EU market.
recommended_actions: Initiate a project to assess the technical requirements for AI content labelling
Engage with EU regulatory bodies to clarify compliance expectations
Develop a timeline for implementing the necessary changes before the regulation takes effect
